title: GOLD 2023 Report: Global Strategy for Prevention, Diagnosis and Management of COPD — Acute Exacerbation Chapter
source: Global Initiative for Chronic Obstructive Lung Disease (GOLD)
description: Evidence-based management of acute exacerbations of COPD (AECOPD) including bronchodilator therapy, systemic corticosteroids, antibiotic selection, controlled oxygen therapy, NIV, and discharge criteria.
recommendations: CONTROLLED OXYGEN: Target SpO2 88-92% in COPD exacerbation — avoid over-oxygenation which suppresses hypoxic drive and worsens hypercapnia
SHORT-ACTING BRONCHODILATORS: Salbutamol (albuterol) 2.5mg nebulized every 20 minutes x 3 doses, then every 1-4 hours; add ipratropium 0.5mg nebulized every 6 hours
SYSTEMIC CORTICOSTEROIDS: Prednisolone 40mg orally daily x 5 days (equivalent to IV methylprednisolone); reduces treatment failure and length of stay

ANTIBIOTICS: Indicated when sputum is purulent (increased volume and purulence); amoxicillin-clavulanate or doxycycline for outpatient; azithromycin or levofloxacin for hospitalized; anti-pseudomonal coverage if risk factors
PSEUDOMONAL RISK FACTORS: Frequent exacerbations (>=4/year), prior Pseudomonas isolation, FEV1 <30% predicted, recent hospitalization — use piperacillin-tazobactam or ciprofloxacin
NON-INVASIVE VENTILATION (NIV): BiPAP indicated for pH <7.35 and PaCO2 >45 mmHg; reduces need for intubation and in-hospital mortality
INTUBATION CRITERIA: NIV failure, severe acidosis (pH <7.25), hemodynamic instability, decreased consciousness, inability to protect airway
METHYLXANTHINES: Theophylline NOT recommended in acute exacerbation — no added benefit and significant toxicity risk
SPUTUM CULTURE: Obtain before antibiotics if hospitalized, especially if purulent or ICU admission anticipated
CHEST RADIOGRAPH: Obtain to exclude pneumonia, pneumothorax, and pleural effusion as alternative or complicating diagnoses
ABG MONITORING: Arterial blood gas at presentation and 1-2 hours after initiating oxygen or NIV to assess response and guide ventilatory support
VENOUS THROMBOEMBOLISM PROPHYLAXIS: LMWH or unfractionated heparin for all hospitalized COPD exacerbations — high VTE risk during acute illness
DISCHARGE CRITERIA: SpO2 stable on baseline oxygen, able to use inhaler independently, no need for parenteral bronchodilators >4 hours, walking ability, adequate home support
PULMONARY REHABILITATION: Begin within 4 weeks of discharge — reduces re-hospitalization and mortality
